description:
To properly set the group owner of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: 
$ sudo chgrp root /etc/gshadow-
rationale:
The <tt>/etc/gshadow-</tt> file is a backup of <tt>/etc/gshadow</tt>, and as such,
it contains group password hashes. Protection of this file is critical for system security.
ocil_clause:
/etc/gshadow- does not have a group owner of
root
ocil:
To check the group ownership of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /etc/gshadow-
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following group-owner: root
fixtext:
Change the group of the file /etc/gshadow- to root by running the following command:
$ sudo chgrp root /etc/gshadow-
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/gshadow- file must be group-owned by root.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/gshadow- file must be group-owned by root.
stig.vuldiscussion: The "/etc/gshadow-" file is a backup of "/etc/gshadow", and as such, contains group password hashes. Protection of this file is critical for system security.
stig.checktext:
Verify the group ownership of the "/etc/gshadow-" file with the following command:

$ sudo stat -c "%G %n" /etc/gshadow-

root /etc/gshadow-

If "/etc/gshadow-" file does not have a group owner of "root", this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Change the group of the file /etc/gshadow- to root by running the following command:

$ sudo chgrp root /etc/gshadow-

title: Verify Group Who Owns Backup gshadow File
